name: Axiom of Specification
guide_hints: # Level 5: The **axiom of specification** or **axiom of restricted comprehension** states that given a set A and a formula Ψ(x), you can construct a set B which contains exactly those elements x of A which satisfy the formula Ψ(x).
So basically it can be used to prove formulas of the form `∃B ∀x x∈B ⇔ (x∈A ∧ Ψ(x))`. I hope you are able to comprehend this formal statement.
We will define a long term to define the **Set Builder Form** which of the type {<variable> ∈ <term> | <formula>}

Type `ct set_builder "{?v1 ∈ ?t1 | ?f1}" ε ?v2 ∀?v1 ?v1 ∈ ?v2 ⇔ (?v1 ∈ ?t1 ∧ ?(v1)f1)`. This long expression might look difficult to understand for you. But you can interpret it easily. The set will be referred to as that variable v2 such that a set v1 belongs to v2 iff v1 belongs to the term t1 and the formula f1 which may contain v1 as free variable must be true.
